name: i18n-team
description: Internationalize the Ekoa app (EN + PT) - reference app research, rotating friendly messages, full app i18n

members.name: reference-researcher
members.prompt:
Very thorough exploration of the i18n/internationalization setup in the _app-reference/ directory (the old Ekoa UI). I need to understand:

1. What i18n library or approach is used (e.g. next-intl, react-i18next, custom)?
2. Where are translation files stored? What format (JSON, TS, etc)?
3. What are ALL the translation keys and their EN and PT values?
4. How does locale switching work? Is there a context provider, hook, or utility?
5. How are translations loaded (static import, dynamic, lazy)?
6. What's the folder structure for i18n files?

Search for: translation files, i18n config, locale files, language switching, useTranslation hooks, t() function calls, any JSON/TS files with translation strings.

Look in: _app-reference/lib/, _app-reference/i18n/, _app-reference/locales/, _app-reference/messages/, _app-reference/app/, _app-reference/components/

Return ALL translation strings found (both EN and PT) in a structured format I can reuse.

members.name: string-cataloger
members.prompt:
Very thorough exploration of all hardcoded user-facing strings in the ekoa/ directory (the active Ekoa UI). I need a complete catalog of every string that needs internationalization.

Search ALL of these locations:
- ekoa/app/ (all pages and layouts)
- ekoa/components/ (all components including builder/, sidebar, dialogs, etc.)
- ekoa/hooks/ (any user-facing messages in hooks)
- ekoa/stores/ (any user-facing strings like default names)
- ekoa/lib/ (especially friendly-messages.ts, i18n.ts, api/client.ts)

For each file, list:
- The file path
- Each hardcoded string (in quotes as it appears in the code)
- What it is (button label, placeholder, title, error message, status text, tooltip, etc.)

Include things like:
- Page titles ("Builder", "Branding", "Settings", etc.)
- Button text ("Send message", "Cancel build", "Start Building", etc.)
- Placeholders ("Describe your app...", "Enter your username", etc.)
- Status messages ("No output yet", "Build in progress...", "App Running", etc.)
- Section headers, tab labels, dialog text
- Error messages
- Sidebar navigation labels
- Quick action titles and prompts
- Any text in the login page

Be thorough - missing strings means untranslated UI. Return results grouped by file.

members.name: i18n-sidebar
members.prompt:
Apply i18n translations to the sidebar component at `/Users/ggomes/dev/ekoa-dev/ekoa/components/sidebar.tsx`.

The i18n infrastructure is already in place:
- Import `useTranslation` from `@/stores/i18n`
- Use `const { nav } = useTranslation();` to get nav translations
- nav.sections has: commandCenter, agents, config
- nav.items has: builder, chat, myArtifacts, companySpace, agentConfig, platform, branding, users, artifactTypes, integrations, resources, appManagement, tunnel

Replace the hardcoded section titles and item labels in the `sections` array with translations. Since `sections` is defined outside the component, you need to move the nav items inside the component or restructure to use the translations reactively.

The approach: Make the `sections` array be computed inside the `Sidebar` component using the translations. Keep the icons and hrefs the same, just replace the labels/titles.

Also replace "Powered by EKOA - v1.0" with a translated version or keep it as-is since it's a brand string.

Do NOT add emoji. Use PT-PT accents for Portuguese.

members.name: i18n-wizard
members.prompt:
Apply i18n translations to the configuration wizard at `/Users/ggomes/dev/ekoa-dev/ekoa/components/builder/configuration-wizard.tsx`.

The i18n infrastructure is already in place:
- Import `useTranslation` from `@/stores/i18n`
- Use `const { wizard } = useTranslation();` to get wizard translations

Available translation keys in `wizard`:
- configureYourBuild, reviewOptions, projectType, freeform, freeformDesc
- loadingTemplates, analyzingRequest, suggested
- qualityRefinements, requirementsAnalysis, requirementsAnalysisDesc
- automatedTesting, automatedTestingDesc, offByDefault, maxIterations
- visualPolish, visualPolishDesc, increaseBuildTime
- startBuilding, note, noteMessage, yourRequest
- planning, testing, polish

Replace ALL hardcoded English strings in the component with the corresponding `wizard.xxx` translation calls:
- "Configure Your Build" -> wizard.configureYourBuild
- "Review the options below before starting your build." -> wizard.reviewOptions
- "Your Request" -> wizard.yourRequest
- "Project Type" -> wizard.projectType
- "Loading templates..." -> wizard.loadingTemplates
- "Analyzing your request..." -> wizard.analyzingRequest
- "Freeform" -> wizard.freeform
- "No template, build from scratch" -> wizard.freeformDesc
- "Suggested" -> wizard.suggested
- "Quality Refinements" -> wizard.qualityRefinements
- "Requirements Analysis" -> wizard.requirementsAnalysis
- "Analyze requirements before building..." -> wizard.requirementsAnalysisDesc
- "Automated Testing" -> wizard.automatedTesting
- "Run automated tests after build..." -> wizard.automatedTestingDesc
- "OFF by default" -> wizard.offByDefault
- "Max iterations:" -> wizard.maxIterations
- "Visual Polish" -> wizard.visualPolish
- "Apply UI improvements after build..." -> wizard.visualPolishDesc
- "Enabling testing or visual polish..." -> wizard.increaseBuildTime
- "Start Building" -> wizard.startBuilding
- "Planning" -> wizard.planning
- "Testing" -> wizard.testing
- "Polish" -> wizard.polish

Do NOT add emoji. Do NOT change the component logic, just replace strings with translation calls.

members.name: i18n-chat-panel
members.prompt:
Apply i18n translations to the chat panel at `/Users/ggomes/dev/ekoa-dev/ekoa/components/builder/chat-panel.tsx`.

The i18n infrastructure is already in place:
- Import `useTranslation` from `@/stores/i18n`
- Use `const { pages, quickActions, chatPanel: chatPanelT } = useTranslation();` (name it chatPanelT to avoid conflict with the component name)

Also import `useI18nStore` to get the locale reactively for the friendly messages:
- `const language = useI18nStore((s) => s.language);`
- Then pass `language` instead of `getLocale()` to `getFriendlyPhaseMessage(phase, language)`

Strings to replace in the component:
1. The quick actions array should use quickActions translations:
   - title: quickActions.landingPage, prompt: quickActions.landingPageDesc (etc. for each quick action)
   Since quickActions is defined outside the component as a constant, move it inside the component or restructure
2. "What do you want to build?" -> pages.builder.whatToBuild
3. "Choose an example below or describe your app to get started." -> pages.builder.chooseExample  
4. "Build in progress..." placeholder -> pages.builder.buildInProgress
5. "Describe your app..." placeholder -> chatPanelT.describeYourApp
6. "Attach file" title -> chatPanelT.attachFile
7. "Cancel build" title -> chatPanelT.cancelBuild
8. "Send message" title -> chatPanelT.sendMessage
9. "Building..." fallback -> pages.builder.buildingInProgress
10. In MessageBubble: "EKOA Agent" -> chatPanelT.ekoaAgent
11. "Build Initiated" -> chatPanelT.buildInitiated
12. "Output available in the right panel." -> chatPanelT.outputAvailable
13. In formatTime: "Just now" -> chatPanelT.justNow, "Xm ago" -> chatPanelT.minutesAgo(n)

Important: The formatTime function needs access to translations. You can either:
- Pass the translations as parameters
- Or make it a hook/component that uses useTranslation

Also: Replace the `getLocale()` import from `@/lib/i18n` with reactive usage of the i18n store for the progress indicator text.

Do NOT add emoji. Do NOT change the component logic beyond what's needed for i18n.

members.name: i18n-side-panel
members.prompt:
Apply i18n translations to the side panel at `/Users/ggomes/dev/ekoa-dev/ekoa/components/builder/side-panel.tsx`.

The i18n infrastructure is already in place:
- Import `useTranslation` from `@/stores/i18n`
- Use `const { sidePanel: sp } = useTranslation();` to get sidePanel translations

Available translation keys in `sidePanel`:
- files, output, preview, desktop, tablet, mobile
- noOutput, noFiles, runningPreview, previewUnavailable, previewWillAppear
- buildingApp, startingApp, previewFailed, thisMayTakeAMoment
- appRunning, appStopped, refreshPreview, openInNewTab, stopApp

Replace ALL hardcoded English strings:
1. Tab names: "Files" -> sp.files, "Output" -> sp.output, "Preview" -> sp.preview
   Note: The tabConfig array is defined outside the component. Move it inside or restructure.
2. Viewport titles: "Desktop" -> sp.desktop, "Tablet (768px)" -> use sp.tablet, "Mobile (375px)" -> use sp.mobile
3. "No files yet" -> sp.noFiles
4. "Building app..." -> sp.buildingApp
5. "Starting app..." -> sp.startingApp
6. "This may take a moment" -> sp.thisMayTakeAMoment
7. "Preview failed" -> sp.previewFailed
8. "No preview available" -> sp.previewUnavailable
9. "Preview will appear here once your app is built and running" -> sp.previewWillAppear
10. "Refresh preview" title -> sp.refreshPreview
11. "Open in new tab" title -> sp.openInNewTab
12. "Stop app" title -> sp.stopApp
13. PreviewStatusBadge: "App Running" -> sp.appRunning, "Building..." -> sp.buildingApp, "Starting..." -> sp.startingApp, "Failed" -> sp.previewFailed, "Stopped" -> sp.appStopped, "No preview" -> sp.previewUnavailable
14. Port display: keep "(Port ${port})" as is (technical info)

Since PreviewStatusBadge is a child component, either pass translations as props or make it use useTranslation() directly.

Do NOT add emoji. Do NOT change the component logic beyond what's needed for i18n.
